messages.content: You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Submit reads q.closed and sends on the jobs channel without holding q.mu.RLock(), so a concurrent Shutdown can flip closed and close(q.jobs) mid-send, producing a data race on q.closed and a possible send-on-closed-channel panic.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Store.Put acquires a read lock (RLock) instead of a write lock (Lock) while inserting into the codes map, so concurrent shorten requests cause a data race and can trigger a fatal concurrent map write.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In AllowN the availability check uses a strict greater-than (`b.tokens > need`) instead of `>=`, an off-by-one that wrongly denies a request that would exactly exhaust the available tokens (e.g. AllowN(5) on a full bucket of capacity 5 returns false).
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Get no longer acquires c.mu, so it reads from (and deletes expired entries out of) the shared items map without synchronization, creating a data race with concurrent Set/Get/DeleteExpired calls.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In GroupSum the accumulator is declared as `var sums map[string]int` (a nil map) instead of `make(map[string]int)`, so the first `sums[key] += v` write panics with "assignment to entry in nil map" for any input that has at least one data row.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Publish reads the shared subs map and sends on subscriber channels without acquiring b.mu (the RLock/RUnlock pair was dropped), creating a data race with Subscribe and Close that can corrupt the map and panic by sending on a channel Close is concurrently closing.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Transfer no longer acquires l.mu.Lock()/Unlock(), so concurrent calls read and mutate the shared balances map and history slice without synchronization, producing a data race, lost updates, and possibly a fatal concurrent-map-write panic.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

The capacity-overflow check in Put uses `c.ll.Len() >= c.capacity` instead of `> c.capacity`, an off-by-one that evicts the least-recently-used entry one insertion too early so the cache only ever holds capacity-1 entries (and 0 when capacity is 1).
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
New allocates the resource channel with capacity len(items)-1, so pre-filling the pool overflows the buffer and the final send blocks forever, deadlocking construction (an off-by-one buffer size).
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

The final-attempt guard uses `if i == attempts` instead of `if i == attempts-1`, an off-by-one that never fires (i only reaches attempts-1), so after the last failed attempt the code still creates a timer and sleeps a full backoff delay (and needlessly blocks on the context) before returning the error.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In deleteRec the prune step drops the !child.terminal guard (checking only len(child.children) == 0), so deleting a longer word also removes a shorter word that ends at an interior node once that node's last child is pruned (e.g. deleting "gopher" also deletes "go").
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In minHeap.Less the tie-break comparison for equal priorities uses `seq > seq` instead of `seq < seq`, reversing insertion order so equal-priority items are dequeued LIFO instead of the documented FIFO order.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
In Fire, the destination state is assigned with m.current = to before the ok check, so a rejected (illegal) event overwrites the current state with the zero value instead of leaving it unchanged.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
In Config.validate the port upper-bound check uses `c.Port > 65536` instead of `c.Port > 65535`, so the invalid port 65536 (one past the maximum 16-bit TCP port) wrongly passes validation.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

The Push full-check uses the wrong boundary, comparing r.count to len(r.buf)-1 instead of len(r.buf), so the buffer starts overwriting (and advancing head) one element too early and silently drops an item before it is actually full.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Len() reads c.ll.Len() without acquiring c.mu, so it races with Put/Get/Delete which mutate the list under the lock, producing an unsynchronized concurrent read (data race) on a type documented as safe for concurrent use.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

ShortestPath pops the frontier node from the back of the slice (queue[len(queue)-1]) instead of the front, so the traversal is last-in-first-out (depth-first) rather than a real BFS queue, and it can return a path that is not the shortest.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

In the render loop the scan index is advanced by a hardcoded 1 (i = body + end + 1) instead of len(closeDelim) which is 2, so only one of the two closing brace characters is consumed and the leftover '}' is emitted as literal text after every placeholder.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.
Put appends to the shared p.items slice without acquiring p.mu, so it races with concurrent Get and Put calls that read and mutate the same slice header and backing array.
You are GuildLM go-review, a Go code-review specialist from the GuildLM Code Guild.

Add appends to the shared items slice without acquiring r.mu, so concurrent Add calls (and Add racing with Query/Len) form an unsynchronized data race that can lose writes, corrupt the slice header, or panic.
